title: NTV Road Show 6 minutes 38 seconds premiere on 16 November
display_title: NTV Road Show 6 minutes 38 seconds premiere on 16 November
wikitext:
[]: NTV Road Show 6 minutes 38 seconds premiere on 16 November             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Oct 15, 2012 11:17 am I'm not completely sure about it, I can't really read Japanese after all, but apparently on 16 November they'll show the first 6 minutes and 38 seconds of Q on NTV : http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-1817.html

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Mon Oct 15, 2012 11:26 am I just sent the text to a translating site. "The world premiere of six minutes and 38 seconds at the beginning of the Q: Rebuild of Evangelion!" It seems as though you are, indeed, correct. EDIT: Although, I'm not sure if it's tomorrow that this is happening. Below colorful post, it reads: 9:00 by November 9 "TV version ordinal Evangelion" and 9:00 by November 16 "Edition: 6 minutes and 38 seconds at the beginning Q TV Evangelion" +: "TV version fracture Evangelion" I don't know what that means.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Oct 15, 2012 11:35 am I think maybe they'll show it after whatever they have planned with T.V. Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 15, 2012 11:37 am Apparently they're going be airing TV 1.0 and TV 2.0 in anticipation of Q, like so: Friday, November 9th at 9 (PM I'd assume) for TV 1.0. Friday, November 16th at 9 (again, PM?) for TV 2.0 plus the first 6 minutes and 38 seconds of Q (which is also being called "TV version" like 1.0 and 2.0 are). That's certainly a difference in marketing the films! I wonder what's the chance of the first 6 minutes being uploaded online or if there's a way to watch online. That's a pretty clever move to air the first 6 minutes the day before the theatrical debut--it'll leave the fans wanting more. My first assumption is maybe Q really would open up with that Asuka versus Angel battle, but at this point it's best not to assume anything. Edit: Curious if the 6 minutes of TV 3.0 will be different from theatrical 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Oct 15, 2012 11:57 am Thanks for the confirmation, SSD. Apparently I'm crazy enough to exchange November with October, I've edited the first post and changed the title.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 15, 2012 12:43 pm Anno uses surprise marketing move! It's super effective! I'm stoked that things are finally on the faster end of moving forward, despite having a quite a ways to go before I can actually see the film in quality, but it's better than nothing! Hanging in limbo for a while there had become a bit dull, though I suppose that's what Shinji's been doing for a while in Unit-01, huh? :P

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Mon Oct 15, 2012 1:00 pm Sachi wrote: though I suppose that's what Shinji's been doing for a while in Unit-01, huh? :P

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Oct 15, 2012 1:25 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: That's certainly a difference in marketing the films! I wonder what's the chance of the first 6 minutes being uploaded online or if there's a way to watch online.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Mon Oct 15, 2012 1:55 pm I'm 99.44% sure that it'll be uploaded within 48 hours of airing.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Oct 15, 2012 2:19 pm svenge wrote: I'm 99.44% sure that it'll be uploaded within 48 hours of airing.

[]:             Quote Post by lo zaffo » Mon Oct 15, 2012 3:24 pm This is a win-win decision for Khara and NTV, most of all for not-JPNese like me let being feed with quite a big morcel of Q sooner than expected. Internet do not fail me!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Oct 15, 2012 3:57 pm Bad news for the me who was doing a pretty good job of keeping my enthusiasm under control to the point of feeling very confident about being able to wait to see the movie for the first time in a theater rather than as a lousy camrip.

[]:             Quote Post by icomeanon4 » Mon Oct 15, 2012 4:14 pm I think even the most sceptical of Eva fans must now admit that Studio Khara has DEFINITELY animated, voiced, and edited at least 6 minutes and 38 seconds of Evangelion 3.0. Let's hope they finish the rest of the movie in time! Who else is thinking 7 glorious minutes of psychedelic CG piano?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 15, 2012 5:28 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: 48 hours? Nonsense.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Oct 15, 2012 6:21 pm Very cool! This possibly explains the lack of traditional advertising, at least in part. If you're going to release 6:38 of the movie ahead of time, a trailer would just be overkill. I'll check it out and not feel guilty. It's not a leak or anything -- if the creators think that showing us this much ahead of time is OK, that's good by me. Here's hoping that the clip ends on such a glorious cliffhanger that showing us ANYTHING more would ruin the surprise...

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Mon Oct 15, 2012 7:00 pm Gundam Unicorn did the same with the last episode showing the first 7 minutes or so. Wow, suddenly I'm stoked.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Oct 15, 2012 7:05 pm My brain is diamonds

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Oct 15, 2012 7:21 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: It will be up within 5 minutes. The capping/encoding/pirating community is a well oiled and efficient machine. Processing power and bandwidth have never been cheaper or faster. HD encodes of 1 hour shows go up within 10-20 minutes after airing. This is barely 7 minutes long. It will be uploaded before anyone has a chance to rewatch it.

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Mon Oct 15, 2012 7:25 pm icomeanon4 wrote: I think even the most sceptical of Eva fans must now admit that Studio Khara has DEFINITELY animated, voiced, and edited at least 6 minutes and 38 seconds of Evangelion 3.0. Let's hope they finish the rest of the movie in time! Who else is thinking 7 glorious minutes of psychedelic CG piano?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Mon Oct 15, 2012 7:34 pm Good marketing move by Khara.
